content: agent2 here: implementing 'Add Built-in CORS Support' - will touch context.go, cors.go (new), mux.go, tree.go. Let me know if you're touching these files too.

Ack. Our features overlap on detecting 'allowed methods for a route' (I need it for CORS preflight + Access-Control-Allow-Methods, you need it for the 405 Allow header). To avoid duplicate/conflicting logic in tree.go: I'm adding a 'methodNames map[methodTyp]string' + 'func GetMethodName(methodTyp) string' to tree.go (explicitly required by my spec), plus a Mux helper to compute allowed methods for a path by probing tree.FindRoute per method with a scratch Context. I will NOT touch Mux.routeHTTP's core 405 dispatch or add a new Allow header there - I'm implementing CORS via EnableCORS()/EnableCORSWithOptions() that install my own MethodNotAllowedHandler (for OPTIONS preflight + Allow-Methods) and a separate cors.go file. If you also add Allow-header logic to the default 405 path in mux.go, please keep it additive (e.g. new field/handler) so we don't clobber each other on save. Shout if you want to share the 'allowed methods for path' helper instead of us each writing one.

Thanks for the detail. Since we don't seem to share a filesystem (my working tree is still clean at HEAD, no allowed_methods.go present), I'll implement my CORS patch independently against the base commit: cors.go (new) with CORS()/CORSOptions/DefaultCORSOptions, mux.go additions EnableCORS/EnableCORSWithOptions/WithCORS + a private '(mx *Mux) allowedMethods(path string) []string' method (probes tree.FindRoute per method with a scratch Context - doesn't touch findRoute internals), and tree.go additive 'methodNames map[methodTyp]string' + 'GetMethodName'. My own MethodNotAllowedHandler override (installed via mx.MethodNotAllowed) sets its own Allow header for the non-preflight 405 case, so it doesn't depend on your MethodNotAllowedHandler() signature change. Should be safe to merge both patches later since I'm not renaming/removing anything and 'allowedMethods' is a method on *Mux (different namespace from your package-level func of the same name). Good luck!
